accounts package

Submodules

accounts.admin module

Admin registrations for the custom News App user model.

class accounts.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]

Bases: UserAdmin

Configure how newsroom users are managed in the Django admin.

add_fieldsets = ((None, {'classes': ('wide',), 'fields': ('username', 'usable_password', 'password1', 'password2')}), ('Newsroom', {'fields': ('email', 'role')}))
add_form

alias of CustomUserCreationForm

fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('Newsroom', {'fields': ('role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')}))
form

alias of CustomUserChangeForm

list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')
list_filter = ('role', 'is_staff', 'is_active')
property media
model

alias of CustomUser

ordering = ('username',)
search_fields = ('username', 'email')

accounts.apps module

Application configuration for the accounts app.

class accounts.apps.AccountsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

Register startup behavior for account-related signals.

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'
name = 'accounts'
ready()[source]

Import signal handlers after Django finishes loading apps.

accounts.constants module

Role constants used throughout the News App permission system.

class accounts.constants.Roles[source]

Bases: object

Canonical role values and human-readable role choices.

CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist')]
EDITOR = 'editor'
JOURNALIST = 'journalist'
READER = 'reader'

accounts.forms module

Forms for creating users and managing reader subscriptions.

class accounts.forms.CustomUserChangeForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: UserChangeForm

Edit custom user fields in the Django admin interface.

Bases: Meta

fields = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'subscribed_publishers', 'subscribed_journalists')

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password': <django.contrib.auth.forms.ReadOnlyPasswordHashField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.TypedChoiceField object>, 'subscribed_journalists': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>, 'subscribed_publishers': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}
declared_fields = {'password': <django.contrib.auth.forms.ReadOnlyPasswordHashField object>}
property media

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

Create a new News App user together with their newsroom role.

Bases: Meta

fields = ('username', 'email', 'role')
model

alias of CustomUser

base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.contrib.auth.forms.UsernameField object>}
declared_fields = {'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>}

Let readers choose publishers and journalists to follow.

alias of CustomUser

widgets = {'subscribed_journalists': <django.forms.widgets.SelectMultiple object>, 'subscribed_publishers': <django.forms.widgets.SelectMultiple object>}

accounts.models module

Database models for authenticated users and their newsroom preferences.

class accounts.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: AbstractUser

Extend Django’s default user with roles and subscription fields.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

articles

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

date_joined

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editor_publications

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

email

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)
get_role_display(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.CharField: role>)
groups

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

class Pizza(Model):

class Pizza(Model):

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

username

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

accounts.services module

Helpers for keeping Django groups aligned with application roles.

accounts.services.ensure_role_groups_exist()[source]

Create the default role groups if they do not exist yet.

accounts.services.sync_user_role_group(user)[source]

Replace the user’s tracked role group with the expected one.

accounts.signals module

Signal handlers that keep custom users aligned with Django groups.

accounts.signals.keep_role_group_in_sync(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]

Refresh the saved user’s Django group membership after each save.

accounts.tests module

Regression tests for account dashboards, login, and logout flows.

class accounts.tests.LogoutViewTests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: TestCase

Verify that the custom logout flow is secure and usable.

setUp()[source]

Create a reader account used by the logout-related tests.

test_dashboard_renders_logout_as_post_form()[source]

The dashboard should render logout as a POST form instead of a link.

test_logout_post_ends_the_session()[source]

Posting to the logout endpoint should end the authenticated session.

accounts.urls module

URL routes for authentication, dashboards, and reader subscriptions.

accounts.views module

Views for authentication, dashboards, and reader subscriptions.

class accounts.views.DashboardView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, TemplateView

Display role-specific account summaries for the signed-in user.

get_context_data(**kwargs)[source]

Add role-based counts that drive the dashboard summary cards.

template_name = 'accounts/dashboard.html'
class accounts.views.GuidedLoginView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginView

Render the custom login template for the newsroom site.

redirect_authenticated_user = True
template_name = 'registration/login.html'
class accounts.views.GuidedLogoutView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LogoutView

Log users out and return them to the homepage.

next_page = '/'
class accounts.views.ReaderRequiredMixin[source]

Bases: RoleRequiredMixin

Limit a view to users who have the reader role.

required_roles = ('reader',)
class accounts.views.ReaderSubscriptionUpdateView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: LoginRequiredMixin, ReaderRequiredMixin, UpdateView

Update the publishers and journalists followed by a reader.

form_class

alias of ReaderSubscriptionForm

form_valid(form)[source]

Persist subscription changes and show a confirmation message.

get_object(queryset=None)[source]

Edit the currently authenticated reader instead of a URL-selected user.

success_url = '/accounts/dashboard/'
template_name = 'accounts/subscriptions.html'
class accounts.views.RoleRequiredMixin[source]

Bases: UserPassesTestMixin

Restrict a view to authenticated users with one of the allowed roles.

handle_no_permission()[source]

Redirect signed-in users back to the dashboard with a helpful message.

required_roles = ()
test_func()[source]

Return True when the current user has an allowed newsroom role.

class accounts.views.SignUpView(**kwargs)[source]

Bases: CreateView

Register a new user account for the News App.

form_class

alias of CustomUserCreationForm

form_valid(form)[source]

Show a success message after the account has been created.

success_url = '/accounts/login/'
template_name = 'accounts/signup.html'

Module contents

User accounts, roles, and subscription management for the News App.
